assertions.reason: `cargo test --test responses_stream_contract -- --nocapture` passed `text_and_reasoning_accumulate`, which consumed created/output_text/reasoning/output_text deltas, observed `current_response().output_text()` advance from `Hello` to `Hello world`, and finalized as `ResponseStreamTerminal::Completed` with final `output_text` `Hello world`.

`cargo test --test responses_stream_contract -- --nocapture` passed `background_resume_skips_seen_events`, which resumed `resp_stream` with `starting_after=3`, surfaced the next unseen delta ` world`, finalized to `Hello world`, and asserted the captured request included both `stream=true` and `starting_after=3`.

`cargo test --test streaming_parser_contract -- --nocapture` passed `fragmented_sse_frames_reassemble_into_same_logical_events`, which fed arbitrarily chunked SSE bytes plus comment/ignored fields and asserted exact reassembly into the expected logical event frames with multi-line `data:` joined by `\n` and no spurious surfaced events.

The assigned mocked runs passed `[DONE]`-terminated transcripts in `text_and_reasoning_accumulate`, `background_resume_skips_seen_events`, and `resume_uses_server_sequence_numbers`; each stream only finalized successfully after a terminal response event (`response.completed`) and none surfaced `[DONE]` as a user event during the observed flows.

`cargo test --test responses_stream_contract -- --nocapture` passed `text_and_reasoning_accumulate`, which asserted cumulative `response.output_text.delta` handling by checking `current_response().output_text()` after successive deltas (`Hello` then `Hello world`) and confirming the completed response exposes the same final `output_text` `Hello world`.

`cargo test --test responses_stream_contract -- --nocapture` passed both `async_consumers_can_abort_without_hanging` and `incremental_delivery_and_abort`, showing async and sync consumers can receive initial events, abort without hanging, and surface abort as `ErrorKind::Transport`; the same test binary also passed normal completion via `text_and_reasoning_accumulate`.

`cargo test --test streaming_parser_contract -- --nocapture` passed `crlf_and_eof_boundaries_do_not_corrupt_sse_parsing`, which fed CRLF, mixed newline, and EOF-edge framing and asserted the parser emitted the same canonical `response.created` and `response.completed` frames without dropping the final event.

commands.observation: The `responses_stream_contract` test binary finished successfully with 6/6 tests passing: `text_and_reasoning_accumulate`, `background_resume_skips_seen_events`, `async_consumers_can_abort_without_hanging`, `incremental_delivery_and_abort`, `unknown_events_and_invalid_ordering_are_deterministic`, and `terminal_failure_and_refusal_states_remain_explicit`.

The `streaming_parser_contract` test binary finished successfully with 3/3 tests passing: `crlf_and_eof_boundaries_do_not_corrupt_sse_parsing`, `fragmented_sse_frames_reassemble_into_same_logical_events`, and `resume_uses_server_sequence_numbers`.

summary: All 7 assigned primary-generation streaming-core assertions were confirmed by passing mocked cargo stream/parser contract tests and their assertion-specific expectations.
